---
title: "Getting started"
description: "Install dependencies and fetch test resources."
order: 0
---

## Fork and clone the repository

The OpenPipeline code is hosted on GitHub. Create your own copy by forking the
[openpipeline repository](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline)
with the **Fork** button, then clone it locally and add the original as an
`upstream` remote.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## HTTPS

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<YOUR USERNAME>/openpipeline.git
cd openpipeline
git remote add upstream https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline.git
```

## SSH

```bash
git clone git@github.com:<YOUR USERNAME>/openpipeline.git
cd openpipeline
git remote add upstream git@github.com:openpipelines-bio/openpipeline.git
```

:::

## Install Viash and Nextflow {#sec-install-viash-nextflow}

You will need [Java 11](https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/downloads/) (or
higher) and [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) installed. OpenPipeline
is built with [Viash](https://viash.io/quickstart/) and
[Nextflow](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/index.html) — see their docs if
either is new to you.

Check both are installed correctly:

```bash
nextflow run hello -with-docker
viash --version
```

## Fetch test resources

OpenPipeline uses shared test resources to test its pipelines. If everything is
installed correctly, fetch them with:

```bash
viash run src/download/sync_test_resources/config.vsh.yaml
```

**Next:** [Project structure →](project-structure.qmd)
